title: FT: LNG supplies from the Gulf nearing a halt as Qatar stops exports
summary: Qatar halted exports after the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and strikes on Ras Laffan. Supplies to Asia are expected to cease within 10 days.
analytical_commentary: Critical disruption of the global supply chain. The loss of Qatari volumes creates a massive deficit.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Extreme. Creates unprecedented demand for Russian LNG as a 'last resort' alternative.

title: Pakistan faces 'dry' terminals as Qatari LNG shipments end
summary: Pakistan's LNG terminals are expected to stop dispatch entirely by the end of the month as the last Qatari cargoes arrived.
analytical_commentary: Pakistan is highly vulnerable due to 99% dependence on Qatari LNG. This will likely lead to severe power blackouts.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: High. Potential for long-term supply contracts if political hurdles are cleared.

title: Bangladesh introduces gas rationing and shuts universities amid LNG shortage
summary: Acute LNG shortage triggered by Middle East disruptions led Bangladesh to ration gas and close educational institutions.
analytical_commentary: Developing economies in Asia are the first to feel the impact, forcing a pivot back to coal.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Medium. Demonstrates the fragility of the current LNG market.

title: Taiwan scrambles for replacement LNG cargoes to avoid power disruption
summary: Taiwan is moving quickly to secure spot cargoes as it faces a supply gap from the Gulf. Reserves may last only until April.
analytical_commentary: Taiwan's energy transition is under threat. Reliance on maritime LNG makes it geographically vulnerable.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Medium. Indirectly strengthens the case for Russian Arctic LNG via NSR.

title: Japan and South Korea turn to coal and nuclear to mitigate LNG shock
summary: Major Asian importers are increasing coal-fired generation and restarting nuclear reactors to counter soaring LNG prices.
analytical_commentary: The clean energy transition is being paused for energy security pragmatism. Spot prices reached 3-year highs.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Low-Medium. Long-term need for diversified gas remains high.

title: Russia considers redirecting LNG exports from Europe to friendly Asia-Pacific nations
summary: Deputy PM Novak stated Russian companies are exploring long-term contracts to divert gas to India, Thailand, and China.
analytical_commentary: Russia is proactively repositioning itself to capture the market gap left by Middle Eastern disruptions.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Extreme. This is a strategic pivot. Success depends on logistics and finalizing deals.

title: U.S. LNG exporters emerge as 'winners' in Asia amid Iran conflict
summary: U.S. gas exporters are benefiting from the war as Asian governments seek dependable alternatives.
analytical_commentary: The U.S. is leveraging the crisis to cement its role as the dominant 'safe' supplier for Asia.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: High (Competitive). Direct competition for market share in Asia.

title: Asia spot LNG prices surge 68% reaching $25/mmBtu
summary: The JKM benchmark saw a massive daily increase due to the U.S.-Iran conflict and Qatari outages.
analytical_commentary: Price volatility is making LNG a high-risk commodity for developing Asia.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: High. High spot prices increase the profitability of Russian spot sales.

title: South Korea downplays supply concerns despite Qatar uncertainty
summary: The Industry Ministry stated that alternative sources are available and Qatari imports account for only 14% of supply.
analytical_commentary: Official optimism masks the underlying pressure on utilities like KEPCO.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Medium. South Korea remains a major market for Sakhalin-2 LNG.

title: Japan holds emergency reserves but seeks spot LNG from JKM
summary: Japanese traders plan to buy spot cargoes to cover Gulf shortfalls while relying on oil and LNG reserves.
analytical_commentary: Japan's diversified strategy provides a buffer, but prolonged conflict will force difficult choices.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Medium. Maintaining relations (Sakhalin-2) is critical for Japan's security.

title: China to offer domestic LNG futures to reflect local demand
summary: China is set to launch yuan-denominated LNG futures in Shanghai to reduce reliance on JKM and Brent.
analytical_commentary: A step towards 'gas sovereignty' and influencing pricing in a volatile market.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: High. Aligns with Russia's interest in de-dollarized trade.

title: Vietnam and Philippines face first major test for new LNG terminals
summary: Newly launched LNG terminals in SE Asia are struggling with high spot prices, testing gas-to-power strategies.
analytical_commentary: The 'gas as a transition fuel' narrative is being challenged by market volatility.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Medium. Russia has expressed interest in supplying these new markets.

title: Thailand's PTT secures 5-year LNG deal with Petronas
summary: Amidst global uncertainty, Thailand secured supply from Malaysia starting in 2028.
analytical_commentary: Intra-regional trade is seen as a way to enhance security and reduce shipping risks.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas:
Low. Regional competition, but highlights trend of stable partners
.

title: Russia-China pipeline gas remains stable despite LNG turmoil
summary: Pipeline imports from Power of Siberia-1 continue to provide a reliable baseline for China's gas demand.
analytical_commentary: The contrast between maritime LNG vulnerability and pipeline stability is stark.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Extreme. Strengthens Russia's hand in negotiating Power of Siberia-2.

title: Australia remains Japan's top LNG supplier, providing 40% of needs
summary: Despite global shocks, the Australia-Japan supply line remains the most critical energy corridor in ATR.
analytical_commentary: Australia is the anchor for Japanese energy security. Any disruption would be catastrophic.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Low (Competitive). Australia is the primary competitor for Russian LNG.

title: LNG market transition: From buyer's advantage to seller's market
summary: Expected 2026 supply surge is being overshadowed by disruptions, keeping prices elevated.
analytical_commentary: The supply glut predicted for 2026 is being deferred. Sellers retain significant leverage.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: High. Prolongs the window of high prices, beneficial for Russian revenues.

title: India eyes more Russian LNG as Middle East supply falters
summary: Indian officials are exploring increased imports from Russia to fill the gap from Qatari force majeure.
analytical_commentary: India's multi-aligned policy allows it to pivot to Russia when other supplies are constrained.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Extreme. Opportunity to lock in long-term deals with India.

title: Strait of Hormuz risk score: Japan and South Korea most vulnerable
summary: Analysis shows Japan and Korea face highest risk due to lack of domestic production and pipelines.
analytical_commentary: Geopolitical risk is now a permanent factor, leading to a premium on safe routes like NSR.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: High. Validates Russia's Northern Sea Route as a strategic alternative.

title: KOGAS and JERA sign LNG swap agreement to manage supply crises
summary: The world's top two LNG buyers are coordinating volumes to ensure stability during disruptions.
analytical_commentary: Buyer alliances are forming to counter producer power and manage logistics.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: Medium. May limit the ability of individual companies to demand extreme premiums.

title: Shell projects 85% growth in global LNG demand by 2050, led by Asia
summary: Industry leaders remain bullish on gas, seeing current disruptions as a bump in the road.
analytical_commentary: Industry leaders remain bullish on gas despite current geopolitical challenges.
importance_for_russian_oil_gas: High. Justifies continued investment in large-scale Arctic projects.
